title: Triangle of Sadness
logline: A male model and his influencer girlfriend join a luxury yacht cruise with the ultra-wealthy, but when the ship sinks, the social hierarchy is completely upended on a deserted island where survival skills matter more than money.
antagonistOrConflict: Class inequality and social power dynamics

actBreaks.description: Carl and Yaya's relationship conflict resolves as they prepare to board the luxury yacht cruise.

validation.ruling: Scene #11 shows Carl and Yaya's reconciliation in their hotel room where Yaya admits to being manipulative and explains her need for financial security - this fundamentally resolves their relationship conflict and transitions the story from their personal drama to the yacht adventure.

actBreaks.description: The yacht explodes from a pirate attack, ending the cruise ship section and launching the survival story.

validation.ruling: Scene #62 shows the survivors washed up on the island beach after the yacht explosion, with Jarmo caressing unconscious Yaya and the group discovering Nelson (the pirate) among them - this irreversibly shifts the story from luxury yacht disaster to island survival with new power dynamics.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script operates as a clear three-act triptych, with each section functioning almost as a separate short film exploring the same themes through different social contexts. Part One examines modern relationships through the lens of economic power dynamics between Carl and Yaya. Part Two escalates to a broader satire of wealth and class aboard the luxury yacht, culminating in literal and metaphorical chaos during the captain's dinner storm sequence. Part Three inverts everything by stranding the characters on an island where previous social hierarchies become meaningless and survival skills determine power. Each section builds the class warfare theme while completely changing the social environment, creating a structure that feels both episodic and cumulative.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing shifts dramatically between sections. Part One moves slowly and intimately through relationship dynamics (scenes 1-11). Part Two accelerates through yacht introductions before exploding into the extended chaos of the storm/dinner sequence (scenes 40-58), which serves as the script's comedic and thematic centerpiece. Part Three starts frantically with the shipwreck aftermath, then settles into a slower burn as new power dynamics emerge, building to the final confrontation between Yaya and Abigail.

incitingIncident.description: A Filipino crew member on a luxury yacht catches the parcel from the helicopter and radios confirmation.

validation.ruling: Scene #12 is just a helicopter lowering a parcel and doesn't shatter the protagonist's ordinary world. Scene #13 shows a Filipino crew member on a luxury yacht catching the parcel and radioing confirmation - this represents Carl and Yaya's transition from their conflict-ridden ordinary world into the luxury yacht environment that will drive the main story.

midpoint.description: The Captain and Dimitry play a drinking card game where Dimitry keeps losing and drinking more.
midpoint.sceneHeading: INT. YACHT / DINING ROOM / CAPTAIN'S TABLE - EVENING

validation.ruling: Scene #50 is just a drinking game between the Captain and Dimitry. Scene #48 shows only the Captain and Dimitry remaining in the dining room as chaos erupts around them, with Dimitry telling Communist jokes while the Captain quotes Mark Twain - this marks the point where the yacht's civilized facade completely breaks down and the story shifts from luxury cruise to survival chaos.

climax.description: Yaya discovers a luxury resort with an elevator built into the cliff and sun chairs on the beach, laughing wildly while Abigail realizes she must play along despite not being thrilled.

validation.ruling: Scene #89 is the climax where Yaya discovers the luxury resort has been there all along and her attitude immediately shifts back to compassionate superiority, causing Abigail to pick up a rock with murderous intent - this is the peak confrontation that resolves the power dynamics and completes Abigail's character arc.

scenes.heading: INT. CASTING AGENCY / WAITING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Fifty half-naked male models wait in line at a casting agency while Lewis Taylor, a Fashion TV host, interviews them with a camera crew.

scenes.heading: INT. CASTING AGENCY / CASTING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: A model stands solemnly in a bare casting room with four panelists and a small dog while laughter filters in from the waiting room.

scenes.heading: INT. CASTING AGENCY / WAITING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Lewis interviews Carl in the waiting line, explaining how expensive brands make models look down on consumers while cheap brands want smiles.

scenes.heading: INT. CASTING AGENCY / CASTING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Carl auditions for the casting panel who recognize him from an old fragrance campaign and have him walk twice before the casting director gives the designer a look.

scenes.heading: INT. CATWALK / RUNWAY SHOW - DAY
scenes.summary: At a tense fashion show opening, a headset girl forces a front-row couple to move seats, causing them to argue before the man gives in.

scenes.heading: INT. CATWALK / RUNWAY SHOW - DAY
scenes.summary: Yaya walks the runway as the opening model wearing massive wings while Carl watches from the back of the room.

scenes.heading: INT. RESTAURANT - EVENING
scenes.summary: Carl and Yaya finish dinner at a restaurant where Carl struggles with whether to pay the bill while Yaya ignores it and does her makeup, leading to escalating tension about gender roles and money.
scenes.turningPointNote: First major conflict establishing the power dynamics and gender role tensions that drive the relationship

scenes.heading: INT/EXT. UBER CAR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In an Uber, Carl tries to discuss money but Yaya calls the topic unsexy, making Carl feel backstabbed and leading to further argument about gender expectations.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL / ELEVATOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Carl and Yaya argue intensely in a hotel elevator and corridor about money, with Yaya claiming she bought him things and Carl accusing her of using him.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL / CORRIDOR - NIGHT 8
scenes.summary: Carl walks down a long hotel corridor past a cleaning lady, initially determined but increasingly filled with doubt about his fight with Yaya.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL / CARL & YAYA’S ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Carl tries to contact Yaya with no response, then she returns after trying to sleep on a chair, and they reconcile with Yaya admitting she's manipulative.

scenes.heading: INT/EXT. HELICOPTER - DAY
scenes.summary: A helicopter hovers over the ocean and lowers a parcel on a wire to someone below.
scenes.turningPointNote: Transition to the yacht setting that will dominate the rest of the story

scenes.heading: EXT. SPEEDBOAT - DAY
scenes.summary: A Filipino crew member on a luxury yacht catches the parcel from the helicopter and radios confirmation.

scenes.heading: EXT. YACHT / DECK - DAY
scenes.summary: The helicopter pulls up the wire and flies away from the cruise ship.

scenes.heading: INT. YACHT / HALLWAYS & KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: The parcel is carried through the yacht's hallways into the galley.

scenes.heading: INT. YACHT / KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: A chef opens the parcel revealing three jars of Nutella while the Chief Stewardess explains in voiceover that cruise success depends on the first hours and last day.

scenes.heading: INT. YACHT / LOBBY - DAY
scenes.summary: Chief Stewardess Paula gives a pep talk to uniformed service staff in the yacht lobby about impressing guests.

scenes.heading: INT. YACHT / FILIPINO CREW CABIN - DAY 23
scenes.summary: Filipino crew members in reflective sunglasses play cards in their below-deck cabin and look up when they hear noise from the service staff above.

scenes.heading: INT. YACHT / CARL AND YAYA’S CABIN - MORNING
scenes.summary: Abigail the toilet manager tries to clean Carl and Yaya's cabin but they ask her to come back later, leading to confusion about timing.

scenes.heading: EXT. YACHT / A DECK / FORWARD - DAY
scenes.summary: Carl photographs Yaya in a bikini on deck when a shirtless crew member appears, making Carl jealous as he notices Yaya smiling at the man.

scenes.heading: INT. YACHT / RECEPTION DESK - DAY
scenes.summary: Carl reports the shirtless, smoking crew member to Chief Stewardess Paula at reception, who apologizes profusely and promises to handle it.

scenes.heading: INT. YACHT / OUTSIDE CAPTAIN’S CABIN - DAY
scenes.summary: Paula knocks on the Captain's cabin door about the safety drill but he says he's not feeling well and asks the First Officer to handle it.

scenes.heading: INT. YACHT / DINING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: At lunch, Dimitry questions why Yaya photographs pasta without eating it, and she claims gluten intolerance while Carl tries to cover the awkwardness.

scenes.heading: EXT. YACHT / A DECK / FORWARD - DAY
scenes.summary: The shirtless crew member is escorted off the yacht onto a speedboat while Carl watches from above.

scenes.heading: EXT. YACHT / BAR / DECK - NIGHT
scenes.summary: At the deck bar, Dimitry points out Finnish businessman Jarmo staring at Yaya and Ludmilla, commenting on his internal struggle about approaching them.

scenes.heading: INT. YACHT / OUTSIDE CAPTAIN’S CABIN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Paula tries again to reach the Captain about the Captain's Dinner but he insists he's not drunk, just unwell, while she presses him for a timeline.

scenes.heading: EXT. YACHT / BAR / DECK - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jarmo orders expensive champagne for Yaya's table and tells Carl to treat the girls right before Dimitry leaves with Ludmilla.

scenes.heading: EXT. YACHT / C DECK / TOP DECK - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A guard patrols the top deck with a machine gun while lounge music plays in the background.

scenes.heading: EXT. YACHT / BAR / DECK - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Yaya tries to get Jarmo to dance but he refuses, claiming he's terrible at it, so she gives up and returns to her seat.

scenes.heading: INT. YACHT / CARL AND YAYA’S CABIN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Yaya returns to find Carl has stuffed pillows in the bed to fake sleeping, and he confronts her from hiding about who she was with.

scenes.heading: EXT. YACHT / A DECK / FORWARD - DAY
scenes.summary: Carl photographs Yaya using boat props while feeling like a stereotypical Instagram boyfriend, as Vera tells stewardess Alicia that people regret working too much on their deathbeds.

scenes.heading: INT/EXT. YACHT / BRIDGE / BRIDGE - DAY
scenes.summary: Paula asks First Officer Darius to tell guests the crew can't swim because the Captain isn't responding, but Darius suggests just letting them swim.

scenes.heading: INT. YACHT / OUTSIDE CAPTAIN’S CABIN - DAY
scenes.summary: Paula knocks repeatedly on the Captain's cabin door with no response while an elderly couple passes by in the hallway.

scenes.heading: INT. YACHT / KITCHEN - DAY 44
scenes.summary: The Chef tells his kitchen staff to get their swimming gear and go to the water slide after receiving a phone call.

scenes.heading: INT. CREW QUARTERS -
scenes.summary: A crewman knocks on doors calling crew members out and scolds two sitting maids to get up.

scenes.heading: INT. YACHT / OUTSIDE CAPTAIN’S CABIN - DAY 46
scenes.summary: The Captain emerges from his cabin in a bathrobe with wet hair just as Paula announces over the PA system that the crew will swim for the guests.

scenes.heading: EXT. YACHT / ENGINE ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: An engineer shouts to another over engine noise to come upstairs.

scenes.heading: EXT. YACHT / REAR DECK - DAY
scenes.summary: The entire crew lines up on the rear deck in swim gear and takes turns going down the water slide while Vera cheers them on.

scenes.heading: INT. YACHT (VARIOUS ROOMS) - EVENING
scenes.summary: Thunder increases and the yacht rocks as objects like curtains, chandeliers, and champagne glasses move ominously in various rooms.
scenes.turningPointNote: Storm begins, setting up the chaos that will dominate the rest of Act 2

scenes.heading: INT. YACHT / DINING ROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: Paula and Darius escort the Captain to greet guests at the delayed Captain's Dinner as everyone bobs with the waves looking drunk.

scenes.heading: INT. YACHT / DINING ROOM (AT TABLE #1) - EVENING
scenes.summary: Guests struggle with the seven-course Cantonese-Swedish fusion menu as seasickness begins, starting with oysters and caviar while Jarmo babbles to the Captain.

scenes.heading: INT. YACHT / DINING ROOM (AT TABLE #2) - EVENING
scenes.summary: Anxiety builds as the third dish of jiggling fish roe is served to Vera, with the yacht's movement making the food appear to move.

scenes.heading: INT. YACHT / HALL - EVENING
scenes.summary: A maid cart rolls down the hall and crashes into the wall as the yacht rocks.

scenes.heading: INT. YACHT / DINING ROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: Paula advises seasick guests to keep eating while china and silverware slide off tables, and she maintains her smile despite the chaos.

scenes.heading: INT. YACHT / DINING ROOM (AT TABLE #3) - EVENING
scenes.summary: The Captain gets a burger while guests receive severed octopus tentacles, and multiple guests begin vomiting into napkins and on plates as they excuse themselves.

scenes.heading: INT. YACHT / DINING ROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: The Captain sits alone at his table finishing wine and drinking from abandoned glasses after all other guests have left.

scenes.heading: INT. YACHT / CABINS & CORRIDORS (VARIOUS) - EVENING 57
scenes.summary: Paula helps violently seasick guests to their rooms as Clementine vomits in the hallway and into Winston's hat.

scenes.heading: INT. YACHT / DINING ROOM - EVENING 58
scenes.summary: Only the Captain and Dimitry remain in the dining room as Dimitry tells Communist jokes and the Captain quotes Mark Twain about arguing with idiots.

scenes.heading: INT. YACHT (VARIOUS ROOMS) - EVENING
scenes.summary: Guests stagger through hallways vomiting in bags, on carpets, stairs, and cabin doors as someone slips in vomit.

scenes.heading: INT. YACHT / DINING ROOM / CAPTAIN'S TABLE - EVENING
scenes.summary: The Captain and Dimitry play a drinking card game where Dimitry keeps losing and drinking more.
scenes.turningPointNote: The two authority figures getting drunk together while chaos reigns sets up the complete breakdown of order

scenes.heading: INT. YACHT / CAPTAIN’S CABIN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Dimitry locks himself in the bridge and repeatedly announces over the loudspeaker that he sells shit.

scenes.heading: INT. YACHT (VARIOUS ROOMS) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Passengers panic throughout the ship as Dimitry announces over loudspeaker that the ship is sinking and they're setting course for Cuba, while the Captain hurries to his office with bottles.

scenes.heading: INT. YACHT / CAPTAIN’S CABIN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Captain tries to stop Dimitry but Dimitry wrestles the microphone away, with the Captain clarifying he's a marxist not communist while Dimitry continues his broadcast.

scenes.heading: INT. YACHT / HALL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Passengers panic and soil themselves throughout the ship while the Captain and Dimitry argue over the loudspeaker about Marx, communism, and tax avoidance.

scenes.heading: INT. YACHT / DINING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Six Filipino cleaning ladies wearing masks methodically clean the dining room mess while one listens to Rage Against The Machine through headphones as she wipes vomit.

scenes.heading: INT. YACHT / CAPTAIN'S CABIN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Captain turns the microphone back on and delivers a speech about rich people justifying their actions while shit flows through the halls.

scenes.heading: INT. YACHT / HALL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Paula and Darius gather at the breaker box and suddenly all the lights go out.

scenes.heading: INT. YACHT / CAPTAIN’S CABIN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In darkness, the Captain reads to Dimitry by cell phone light about his childhood memories of Martin Luther King and Bobby Kennedy being killed.

scenes.heading: INT/EXT. PIRATE SPEEDBOAT - DAWN
scenes.summary: Pirates in a speedboat with machine guns pass a hand grenade while the Captain's political speech echoes over the water.

scenes.heading: EXT. YACHT / DECK / PORTSIDE - EARLY MORNING
scenes.summary: A hand grenade rolls across the yacht deck to Clementine, who picks it up thinking it's theirs while Winston realizes the danger.

scenes.heading: EXT. SEA - EARLY MORNING
scenes.summary: The yacht explodes from the grenade in a long shot view from the sea.
scenes.turningPointNote: The yacht is destroyed, ending Act 2 and beginning the island survival story

scenes.heading: EXT. ISLAND / BEACH - DAY
scenes.summary: Jarmo sits with unconscious Yaya on the beach and caresses her cheek until he hears voices and sees other survivors dragging a life raft with Therese and Nelson.

scenes.heading: EXT. ISLAND / CAMP - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The group huddles together on an inflatable life raft in a clearing surrounded by trees, unable to sleep due to hunger and thirst in the pitch darkness.

scenes.heading: EXT. ISLAND / BEACH - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The survivors reach the beach, fall to their knees praying in various languages while someone lights their last emergency flare despite Paula's protests.

scenes.heading: EXT. ISLAND / BEACH - DAY
scenes.summary: Carl wakes up rattled and notices Yaya's face covered in sand flea bites while he has none, then sees Jarmo yelling and discovers something that makes them all run.

scenes.heading: INT/EXT. LIFEBOAT - DAY
scenes.summary: The survivors find Abigail, a Filipino cleaning lady, in a lifeboat with supplies and she begins handing out water and chips to the desperate group.
scenes.turningPointNote: Abigail emerges as the person with resources and skills, shifting the power dynamic

scenes.heading: EXT. ISLAND / BEACH - DAY
scenes.summary: The group watches Abigail catch an octopus with her bare hands while they eat potato chips, and she announces they need to make a fire.

scenes.heading: EXT. ISLAND / CAMP - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Around the campfire, Abigail portions out the cooked octopus but keeps a large portion for herself, arguing with Paula that she deserves more because she did all the work.
scenes.turningPointNote: Abigail establishes her dominance by controlling food distribution based on labor contribution

scenes.heading: INT. ISLAND / BEACH / LIFEBOAT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Dimitry and Jarmo plead outside the lifeboat window, dangling their expensive watches while Yaya, Paula and Abigail gather towels inside.

scenes.heading: EXT. ISLAND - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Carl discovers a bag of pretzels in Abigail's backpack and after hesitating, tells Nelson and Therese about it, leading them to eat from it.

scenes.heading: EXT. ISLAND / BEACH - MORNING
scenes.summary: Dimitry drags Vera's corpse from the water onto the beach, weeps over her, then removes and pockets her diamond necklace.

scenes.heading: EXT. ISLAND / CAMP - MORNING
scenes.summary: Abigail confronts Carl and Nelson about letting the fire go out and discovers someone ate from her backpack, threatening punishment for the theft.

scenes.heading: EXT. ISLAND / MAIN BEACH - DAY
scenes.summary: Carl searches through shipwreck debris and finds a bottle of cologne from his old modeling campaign.

scenes.heading: EXT. ISLAND / MAIN BEACH - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Paula, Yaya, and Abigail walk down the beach carrying multiple fish that Abigail caught while Dimitry runs to catch up and compliment her fishing skills.

scenes.heading: EXT. ISLAND / CAMP - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The group eats around the campfire while Carl and Nelson are denied food as punishment, and Yaya notices Carl staring at Abigail rather than at her.

scenes.heading: EXT. ISLAND / BEACH - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Yaya sits upset on a rock while Carl runs to her with pretzels, and they argue about his relationship with Abigail before she calls him to the lifeboat.

scenes.heading: EXT. ISLAND / BEACH - MORNING
scenes.summary: Carl climbs out of the lifeboat in the morning and encounters Yaya, who calls him an asshole before walking away.

scenes.heading: EXT. ISLAND / BEACH - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: A montage shows Carl repeatedly exiting the lifeboat after spending nights with Abigail, becoming more relaxed about hiding the pretzel sticks over time.

scenes.heading: EXT. ISLAND / BEACH - DAY (PART OF MONTAGE)
scenes.summary: Jarmo and Therese sit on the beach discussing Carl and Abigail's relationship, with Nelson asking Therese if she ever used sex to gain advantages when younger.

scenes.heading: EXT. ISLAND / HIGH GRASS AREA - DAY
scenes.summary: Nelson investigates rustling bushes and hears an animal cry before retreating to the others, then Jarmo takes a sharpened stick to hunt whatever they found.

scenes.heading: EXT. ISLAND / FLAT CLIFF WALL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The group gathers around a fire facing a rock wall where Jarmo gives a speech about killing and painting a donkey, critiquing Yaya's charcoal drawing.

scenes.heading: INT. ISLAND / BEACH / LIFEBOAT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Carl and Abigail argue in the lifeboat about their sexual arrangement, with Carl calling her a flesh peddler and Abigail defending their transactional relationship.

scenes.heading: EXT. SHORE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Nelson shaves Dimitry's face on the beach while they discuss piracy as a business, with Nelson revealing he makes 3000 euros per successful attack.

scenes.heading: INT. ISLAND / BEACH / LIFEBOAT - DAY
scenes.summary: Yaya knocks on the lifeboat hatch where Carl and Abigail are naked together, asking to borrow the backpack for a hike, with Abigail insisting on joining her.

scenes.heading: EXT. ISLAND / JUNGLE PATH - DAY
scenes.summary: Yaya leads through thick vegetation with a machete while Abigail follows, praising Yaya for creating a matriarchy and domesticating the alpha males.

scenes.heading: EXT. ISLAND / STEEP MOUNTAIN - DAY
scenes.summary: Yaya and Abigail reach a steep mountain peak with Abigail growing weary, and Yaya disappears around a corner ahead of her.

scenes.heading: EXT. ISLAND / CAMP (& BEACH) - DAY
scenes.summary: Therese alone in the beached lifeboat spots a beach vendor walking with bags, cloth, sunglasses and Panama hats through the foliage.

scenes.heading: EXT. ISLAND / STEEP HILLSIDE - DAY
scenes.summary: Yaya and Abigail move slowly on a steep, dangerous hillside above crashing waves when Yaya shouts excitedly about seeing something.

scenes.heading: EXT. ISLAND / UNEXPLORED BEACH - DAY
scenes.summary: Yaya discovers a luxury resort with an elevator built into the cliff and sun chairs on the beach, laughing wildly while Abigail realizes she must play along despite not being thrilled.
scenes.turningPointNote: The discovery of civilization threatens to end Abigail's power and return everyone to their original social positions

scenes.heading: EXT. ISLAND / JUNGLE PATH - DAY
scenes.summary: Carl runs as fast as he can through the woods.

structuralWarnings.text: 2 of 3 key moments were auto-corrected by Phase 1C
